courseOutcomes: CO1: Understand object-oriented programming concepts and principles
CO2: Implement classes, objects, and inheritance patterns
CO3: Apply polymorphism and abstraction in real-world scenarios
CO4: Design and develop complex OOP applications
instructions: Write pseudocode or actual code where required
Diagrams should be clear and labeled

questions.text: What is a class? How is it different from an object? Explain with a real-world example.
Define encapsulation and explain its importance in OOP.

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Explain access modifiers (public, private, protected) with examples

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Write a Java class demonstrating getters and setters for encapsulation

questions.text: Explain inheritance in OOP. What are the types of inheritance?

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Design a class hierarchy for a banking system with Account, SavingsAccount, and CheckingsAccount classes

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Explain multiple inheritance problem and how interfaces solve it in Java. Provide code example.

questions.text: What is polymorphism? Explain method overloading and method overriding.

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Write Java code demonstrating both compile-time (method overloading) and runtime (method overriding) polymorphism

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Design a Shape class hierarchy with Circle, Rectangle, and Triangle. Implement area calculation using polymorphism.

questions.text: What is abstraction? How do abstract classes and interfaces differ in achieving abstraction?

orOptions.label: A
orOptions.text: Create an abstract Animal class with concrete subclasses Dog and Cat. Demonstrate abstract methods.

orOptions.label: B
orOptions.text: Design an interface-based solution for a Vehicle system with Car, Bike, and Truck implementations
